name: OpenClaw Agent Harness
description: Multi-agent development harness: crystallise -> plan -> execute (Sonnet) -> adversarial review (Fable-5) -> PR.

listener_enabled.description: DEPRECATED as of beta.34: IGNORED. The Slack listener was removed; the harness is tool-driven only (drive it via harness_run / harness_start_session / harness_merge_pr). A `true` value is logged and does nothing. This key is retained only for config back-compat.

channel.description: Slack channel ID (e.g. C0DEVCHAN). Only required when listener_enabled is true.

authorised_users.description: Slack user IDs allowed to open sessions or drop control reactions

credential_service.description: Vault service name for the Slack bot token. Enables reactions poller.

daily_max_usd.description: Hard daily spend ceiling (USD); basis for the deploy-repair budget. Must be >= daily_warn_usd.

allowed.description: Repos the harness can touch. Supports owner/repo or owner/* glob.

price_overrides.description: Per-model price overrides for cost estimation, keyed by model id. Values are USD per million tokens.

auth.description: Anthropic auth for the embedded Claude Agent SDK. Vault-first (credential_service), env fallback (api_key_env, default ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).

lead_timeout_seconds.description: beta.43: max seconds the lead-planner SDK call may run before it is treated as a hang and the run fails cleanly (loop.lead_timeout). The lead await was unbounded (beta.42 only bounded the worker); a healthy long plan was indistinguishable from a wedge.

stuck_loop_seconds.description: beta.40: reclaim threshold for a wedged loop. If run() is asked to start a session still marked running by the module-level re-entrancy guard, but its last_checkpoint_at/updated_at has not advanced for this many seconds, the tracked loop is treated as dead (torn down with a prior runtime on plugin re-register), the stale guard entry is force-cleared, and the fresh run proceeds. Must exceed a normal long worker SDK call so a busy loop is never reclaimed.

teardown_drain_seconds.description: beta.41: max seconds teardown() waits for a still-running loop from the runtime being torn down to finish before closing its state DB. A plugin re-register (OKF / gateway auto-discovery churn when plugins.allow is empty) schedules a fire-and-forget teardown of the previous runtime; closing the DB out from under an in-flight loop.run() crashes the run. Draining first prevents that. Bounded so a wedged loop can't block teardown forever.

stall_watchdog_seconds.description: beta.42: active stall-watchdog delay. When the re-entrancy guard skips a re-entry (loop.run_skipped_already_running) it arms a timer for this long, then re-checks the session's progress; if none, the wedged loop's stale guard handle is force-deregistered (loop.wedge_detected) so recovery/next-run can reclaim it. Makes beta.40's passive reclaim active -- a loop that wedges with no further re-register is now noticed.

subtask_deadline_seconds.description: beta.60: max wall-clock seconds a single sub-task's dispatch (runOne) may run before it is force-failed. beta.42 bounded only the worker SDK call, but runOne also awaits unbounded git/IO (worktreeHeadSha, readReactions, verify probes, recordSpend) between the row-flip-to-running and the worker spawn; a hang in any of those wedged the whole dispatcher (b59 PR#858 seq-7: 5h30m silent, row stuck running with sdk_session_id=null and no worker process). This bounds the entire runOne invocation. Must be >= worker_timeout_seconds plus IO margin.

budget_reserve_ratio.description: beta.61: fraction of the total session budget held in reserve for the pending adversary review + push while a cycle's review hasn't run yet. The pre-sub-task projection adds this reserve so the loop aborts early (before a sub-task that would leave no room to finish the cycle) instead of completing all sub-tasks and dying one review short of a PR (the b60 smoke failure). Clamped [0,0.9].

env_wait_retry_enabled.description: beta.53 (P1b): when a worker ends its turn awaiting a non-existent mid-turn 'Monitor event' (env-wait hallucination) and made no committed change, re-invoke the sub-task ONCE with corrective context (branched on whether it wrote-but-didn't-commit) instead of failing the run. Set false to disable the retry; the failure is still tagged loop.worker_env_wait_hallucination.

clarification_escalation_enabled.description: beta.55 (B2): when a worker refuses/confabulates a sub-task even after the beta.54 async-coord retry, pause the session in 'awaiting_clarification' (persisting the worker's question + paused seq, preserving the worktree) and surface it via harness_progress for a human to answer with harness_answer, instead of hard-failing the run. Set false to keep the terminal-fail behaviour.

graceful_pr_on_review_crash.description: beta.62 (fix #2/#3): when a cycle-N adversary review CRASHES (SDK/parse/persist error) instead of returning a verdict, and a PRIOR cycle already produced a completed review AND this cycle's own sub-task self-verification was fully green, open the PR anyway with merge_recommendation='needs_human_review' rather than discarding the adversary-motivated commits (the b60-attempt-2 silent-review-crash failure: 8 good commits, seq-6 self-verify green, cycle-2 review crashed, work thrown away). When the graceful path is NOT taken, the worktree is preserved (not released) so the commit chain stays inspectable. Set false to keep the hard-fail-and-release behaviour.

session_stall_seconds.description: beta.63 (Part A): session-level stall watchdog window. A session writes session.last_progress_at on EVERY state transition; the watchdog checks non-terminal executing/reviewing/finalising sessions where now-last_progress_at exceeds this and emits a loud loop.session_stalled, attempts bounded self-recovery (re-tick the loop-runner), and if unrecoverable transitions to a terminal failed(reason=stalled_no_progress) PRESERVING the worktree and, if the branch has commits, opening a graceful push+PR flagged needs_human_review. Must exceed the longest legit phase (adversary review + push); clamped to >=300.

stall_auto_terminal.description: beta.63 (Part A): sub-flag gating the AUTO-TERMINAL transition of a stalled session. When false, the watchdog still DETECTS + LOGS + attempts recovery, but never forces the terminal failed transition (detection/observability on, auto-transition off, kept separately toggleable per Carel).

stall_graceful_pr.description: beta.63 (Part A): on an UNRECOVERABLE stall with commits on the branch, attempt a graceful push + PR flagged needs_human_review (beta.62 pattern) so a near-done deliverable is not evaporated. Set false to fail-and-preserve-worktree only.

stall_sweep_interval_seconds.description: beta.67 (Bug A): EXTERNAL stall-sweep cadence (seconds). beta.63's checkStalls runs IN-PROCESS, so a dead loop-runner process cannot watchdog its own death (beta.66 smoke #4). This is the tick interval for the EXTERNAL stall-sweep service (registered like pr-watcher / retention-nightly) that runs loop.sweepStalls() independent of any loop process: it runs the existing checkStalls fast path AND reaps sessions with a pending cancel flag whose loop is dead (terminal failed reason=cancelled_dead_loop, worktree preserved). Default 60; clamped [15,600].

enforce_worker_context.description: beta.67 (P0a): enforce SUBSTANTIVE workerContext (rationale + file-anchored changeSpec/excerpt) on mutate/mixed sub-tasks at validatePlan. true (default) -> one bounded lead re-ask then hard-throw; false -> WARN-only escape hatch. Enforces the founding orchestrator-split goal (cheap workers implement from Fable's investigation instead of re-scanning).

revise_spec_turn_enabled.description: beta.67 (P0b): run ONE Fable revise-spec turn between the adversary and cycle-2 workers to refresh workerContext with a resolved changeSpec, so workers never see the raw adversary findings (the beta.63/64 no-op regression). false -> beta.66 raw-findings behaviour; a revise-spec failure also falls back. Default true.

sdk_first_token_timeout_seconds.description: beta.64 (P0-1) / beta.65 (P0): PHASE-2 first-token watchdog window (seconds). Armed inside consumeWorkerStream when the SDK stream OPENS (system/init) and disarmed on the first assistant content block (text/tool_use). No first content block within this window => abort with the distinct stopReason first_token_timeout so the loop retries on a fresh session. beta.65 split-phase redesign: phase 2 (stream-open -> first-token) is always near-instant on success (4-5ms in smoke #3), so the default is LOWERED 90 -> 30. The pre-stream stall is covered by sdk_stream_open_timeout_seconds (phase 1). Clamped [10,1800].

sdk_stream_open_timeout_seconds.description: beta.65 (P0): PHASE-1 stream-open watchdog window (seconds). A SEPARATE timer armed at CALL INITIATION (top of consumeWorkerStream, BEFORE the stream opens) and disarmed when the stream opens (system/init). If the stream never opens within this window, the call is aborted with the same distinct stopReason first_token_timeout so the loop retries on a FRESH SDK session. Covers the beta.64 gap: a PRE-STREAM POST hang (SDK streaming POST never returns its first byte -- smoke #3: 28+min silence, no sdk_stream_opened, no abort) that beta.64's stream-open-armed watchdog never saw. Phase 1 is highly variable even on success (smoke #3: 47s / 422s-succeeded / >1800s-hung), so a legit-but-slow open like 422s WILL be aborted -- CORRECT, because the abort routes into the same first_token_timeout -> one-fresh-session retry path (a cold/unpooled open is fast on retry). Clamped [10,600].

worker_timeout_retry_enabled.description: beta.64 (P0-2): when a worker sub-task fails with a first_token_timeout OR a worker timeout, RETRY it ONCE on a FRESH SDK session (no resumeSessionId) before flipping the run terminal. The retry re-verifies; a pass completes the sub-task, a fail falls through to the existing terminal path using the retry's result. Max 1 retry per sub-task.

best_effort_verify.description: beta.64 (P0-3): BEST-EFFORT VERIFY. If a VERIFY sub-task (observe-mode, the last/verify sub-task) times out even after the P0-2 retry, AND the prior mutate sub-task's verify_probe was GREEN, AND git diff-stat shows only expected files touched, mark the run verify_skipped (reason worker_timeout), push the branch, and open the PR flagged merge_recommendation=needs_human_review (reusing the beta.62 graceful-PR machinery) rather than discarding shippable work. This is what SHOULD have happened in beta.63 smoke #2 -- the code was shippable, only the verifier hung.

scripted_verify_fallback.description: beta.64 (P0-4): SCRIPTED VERIFIER FALLBACK. When an observe-mode VERIFY sub-task times out (before giving up to best-effort verify), run a DETERMINISTIC fallback -- npx tsc --noEmit + git diff --stat <base>..HEAD + the allowlisted repo check scripts (reusing beta.63 runFinalVerifyChecks/discoverCheckScripts/runCheckScripts) -- and report pass/fail to the loop as if the sub-task ran. A run-tsc/lint/diff/grep verify sub-task needs no LLM, so a hung verifier should not block a shippable change.

api_key_env.description: Env-var fallback for the Vercel token when the vault is unavailable or empty.

deploy_repair.description: beta.36: post-merge deploy-repair loop. On a merged PR whose Vercel deploy ERRORs, auto-attempt fixes (up to max_attempts new PRs) from the build logs; if still failing, revert all merges and leave the last attempt open for review.

budget_ratio.description: Repair budget as a fraction of budgets.daily_max_usd, shared across all attempts.

pat_routing.description: Git credential routing. beta.25 hierarchical shape (github/gitlab -> org -> person -> {token,name,email,slack_user_id}) plus legacy flat fields. See docs/GITHUB_AUTH.md.

github.description: Hierarchical GitHub routing: org -> person -> {token:{value|env|vault}, name, email, slack_user_id}.

gitlab.description: Hierarchical GitLab routing: org -> person -> {token:{value|env|vault}, name, email, slack_user_id}.

commit_identity.description: Legacy per-user commit identity (superseded by hierarchical name/email).

logging.description: Harness log verbosity. 'debug' makes error sites log full error objects.

brief.description: beta.63 (convention-awareness Fix 1): brief construction / repo-convention ingest tuning.

ingest_repo_conventions.description: beta.63 (Fix 1): at brief build, ingest the checked-out repo's declared convention files (.cursor/rules/**, .cursorrules, CONTRIBUTING.md, CONVENTIONS.md, AGENTS.md, .github/CONTRIBUTING.md) into the optional brief field repoConventions[] so the lead + worker + adversary SDK prompts (which get NO OpenClaw context injection) explicitly carry them.

convention_char_budget.description: beta.63 (Fix 1): total char budget for the ingested conventions block. Over budget, the LONGEST sources are truncated first with a note, rather than dropping sources silently.

run_repo_check_scripts.description: beta.63 (Fix 2): the final-verify sub-task runs repo-declared check scripts (from package.json#scripts) inline + blocking in the worktree. A non-zero exit becomes a REVISE-worthy loop.convention_check_failed finding, NOT a hard run-fail (the code may be correct and only a bundle stale). An unrunnable / network-needing script is logged non-fatal + noted.

check_script_allowlist.description: beta.63 (Fix 2): allowlist of package.json script names the harness may run in final-verify. A discovered script NOT on this list is NEVER run.

check_script_timeout_seconds.description: beta.63 (Fix 2): per-check-script wall-clock timeout (seconds). A timed-out script is treated as unrunnable (non-fatal log + note), not a hard fail.

log.description: beta.63 (Part B): durable, structured, append-only interaction log written OUTSIDE the git worktree (in the harness data dir). Captures every SDK/LLM call, state transition, verify probe, and stall/recovery event so a near-completion failure leaves a complete, greppable trail that survives a worktree release + container restart. Secret redaction on write is mandatory and NOT disableable.

interaction_log_enabled.description: beta.63 (Part B): master switch for the interaction log.

dir.description: beta.63 (Part B): directory for the JSONL logs. Default <dataDir>/logs where dataDir is the directory holding the state DB. Leave empty for the default.

full_prompts.description: beta.63 (Part B): when false (DEFAULT) only prompt SIZES + TAILS are logged, not full prompt bodies (transcripts can be huge + sensitive). Set true for deep-debug. This does NOT disable secret redaction — redaction on write is always applied.

retention_days.description: beta.63 (Part B): prune per-session log files older than this many days.
